original_title: Overall Survival with [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 Versus [177Lu]Lu-PSMA I&T: A Propensity Score-Matched Real-World Analysis
title: Lu-177 PSMA-617 and PSMA I&T, matched head to head on survival
blurb: A propensity-matched real-world comparison of the two Lu-177 PSMA ligands on overall survival, which is the question the registrational trials never ran directly against each other. Matching is not randomization and the usual real-world confounders apply, but it is the closest read we have on whether the ligand choice actually moves the outcome.

original_title: AI-based tumor and organ volumetry predicts early progression after Y-90 radioembolization in liver-dominant metastatic disease
title: AI volumetry to call Y-90 radioembolization failure earlier
blurb: Automated tumor and organ segmentation used to flag early progression after Y-90 radioembolization in liver-dominant disease, where manual RECIST struggles with multifocal tumor. It is a retrospective model on their own cohort, so the predictive value needs a prospective test before it changes how anyone follows these patients.
venue: Eur. J. Nucl. Med. Mol. Imaging

original_title: PSMA PET/CT-Targeted Biopsy in Men with Negative or Equivocal Multiparametric MRI and Exploratory Dynamic Total-Body PET: The FUPERMAN Study
title: PSMA PET to aim the biopsy when the MRI is equivocal
blurb: The FUPERMAN study points a PSMA PET/CT at the men whose multiparametric MRI came back negative or equivocal, to decide where to biopsy. PET-guided biopsy still has to beat template sampling on its own terms, but the imaging-picks-the-target idea is the direction the field keeps moving.

original_title: Real-world evaluation of [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 RLT in post-taxane mCRPC: feasibility and safety of individualized treatment in a challenging cohort
title: Lu-177 PSMA-617 in the messy post-taxane patients, not the trial-clean ones
blurb: A real-world look at individualized Lu-177 PSMA-617 radioligand therapy in post-taxane mCRPC, the challenging cohort that trials tend to screen out. Feasibility and safety only, from one center, so read it as can-we-do-this rather than does-it-work.
venue: Eur. J. Nucl. Med. Mol. Imaging

original_title: Toward a Biopsy-Free Diagnosis of Prostate Cancer: Potential of Combined 18F-Flotufolastat PSMA PET and mpMRI
title: Pairing 18F-flotufolastat PSMA PET and MRI to skip the biopsy
blurb: The pitch is that combining 18F-flotufolastat PSMA PET with multiparametric MRI could get close to a biopsy-free prostate cancer diagnosis. Biopsy-free is a high bar and the title leans on the word potential, so this is a direction-of-travel paper, not a practice change.

original_title: PET imaging of atherosclerotic plaques using the stabilin-2-targeted tracer 18F-S2P in preclinical models
title: A stabilin-2 PET tracer for atherosclerotic plaque, still in animals
blurb: 18F-S2P targets stabilin-2 to try to light up atherosclerotic plaque on PET, tested here in preclinical models. New tracers look clean in mice and then meet human blood pool and background, so this is one to file under promising if it translates.
venue: Eur. J. Nucl. Med. Mol. Imaging

original_title: Development and Advantages of O-(Carboxymethyl)-L-tyrosine-Based PSMA-Targeting Theranostics for Prostate Cancer
title: Rebuilding the PSMA ligand backbone for a cleaner theranostic
blurb: A PSMA-targeting theranostic built on an O-(carboxymethyl)-L-tyrosine scaffold, pitched as having advantages over the standard motifs. It is a design-and-characterization paper, so the case rests on the binding and biodistribution numbers rather than any patient outcome yet.

original_title: Conformationally Tuned Cyclic RGD Peptides for Integrin-Subtype-Selective PET/CT Imaging
title: Tuning cyclic RGD peptides to tell the integrin subtypes apart
blurb: Conformational tweaks to cyclic RGD peptides aimed at integrin-subtype selectivity for PET/CT, which matters because the pan-integrin tracers blur exactly the distinction you want. Selectivity in a binding assay is the easy part, and holding it in vivo is the part worth watching.

original_title: Glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor PET in indolent and aggressive insulinoma: Diagnostic performance, quantitative differences, and clinical implications
title: GLP-1 receptor PET tries to grade insulinomas, not just find them
blurb: GLP-1 receptor PET for insulinoma, looking at whether quantitative uptake separates indolent from aggressive tumors rather than only locating them. Grading by tracer uptake is a nice idea that usually needs a bigger series before the cutoffs mean anything.
venue: Eur. J. Nucl. Med. Mol. Imaging

original_title: PET-based assessment of coronary microvascular dysfunction and its relation to plaque burden and vascular remodeling: integration with microvascular resistance reserve
title: Quantitative PET for coronary microvascular dysfunction, tied to plaque
blurb: Quantitative myocardial blood flow PET used to pin down coronary microvascular dysfunction and relate it to plaque burden and remodeling, by way of microvascular resistance reserve. It is an association study, so it maps the relationship without telling you yet whether acting on the PET number changes anything.
venue: Eur. J. Nucl. Med. Mol. Imaging

original_title: RIMTAC: A Novel Degrader Design Platform by Indirect VHL-Recruitment via RIPK1
title: A degrader that recruits VHL the long way round, through RIPK1
blurb: RIMTAC is a targeted-degradation design that reaches the VHL machinery indirectly by way of RIPK1 instead of binding VHL directly. Novel wiring for a PROTAC-style tool, and whether the indirection buys real selectivity or just adds moving parts is the thing to judge from the data.

original_title: Dysregulation of Human ClpP Using Small Molecules with Piperazine-Based Scaffold for Diffuse Intrinsic Pontine Glioma Therapy Validated by Patient-Derived Tumor Organoids
title: Small molecules that throw ClpP off, tested on DIPG organoids
blurb: A piperazine-scaffold series that pushes the ClpP protease out of its normal regulation as a way at diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma, with patient-derived tumor organoids doing the validation. Organoids beat a cell line and still are not a child, so it is an honest early step against a tumor with almost nothing that works.

original_title: Rational Design of Diaryl Ether-Based Dual Inhibitors Targeting Successive Essential Enzymes HadAB and InhA in Mycobacterium tuberculosis
title: One molecule hitting two enzymes in the TB fatty-acid pathway
blurb: Rational design of diaryl ethers that inhibit HadAB and InhA, two consecutive enzymes in the same mycobacterial pathway, on the theory that hitting both raises the bar for resistance. Sensible logic, though dual inhibitors often trade away potency at each target, so the balance in the numbers is what matters.

original_title: Catalytically Promiscuous PLP-Dependent Aminotransferases Are Biocatalysts for C-C Bond Formation
title: Aminotransferases moonlighting as C–C bond-forming biocatalysts
blurb: Common PLP-dependent aminotransferases turn out to be promiscuous enough to make carbon-carbon bonds, which is not their day job. Catalytic promiscuity like this is where a lot of useful biocatalysis starts, though getting from a demonstrated side reaction to a practical route is usually a long road.

original_title: Synthesis Based on Covalent Capture and Release Enables Purification-free Fluorogenic Probe Libraries for Single-Molecule Protease Activity Profiling
title: Purification-free probe libraries for single-molecule protease profiling
blurb: A covalent capture-and-release synthesis that spits out fluorogenic probe libraries without the usual purification, aimed at profiling protease activity one molecule at a time. The synthesis trick is the real contribution, and single-molecule readouts reward exactly this kind of cheap, broad probe set.

original_title: Structure-Activity Relationship of Cycling Molecular Assemblies for Golgi Targeting and Disruption
title: Structure-activity work on assemblies that target and wreck the Golgi
blurb: An SAR study on self-assembling molecules built to home to the Golgi and disrupt it, mapping which structural changes drive the effect. Organelle-targeted disruptors are a crowded idea, so the value is in whether the SAR is clean enough to design the next one rather than screen for it.

original_title: Accessing thiophene-fused furans via visible light photoredox-catalyzed [3+2]-annulation reaction
title: A photoredox [3+2] route to benzothiophene-fused furans
blurb: Visible-light photoredox annulating benzothiophenone-derived diazo compounds with alkynes to build fused furan cores in one step. Clean way onto a scaffold that is otherwise a slog, with the standing caveat that diazo starting materials keep some people out.

original_title: Photo-generated radical mediated molecular luminescence enhancement
title: UV light switching a dim benzil into a bright emitter
blurb: Irradiating benzil derivatives jumps the photoluminescence quantum yield from about 1% to 50%, which they trace to UV-generated aromatic carbonyl radicals. A striking number, and the open question is how stable that radical-driven state stays once you stop shining light on it.

original_title: Hidden interfacial electric fields in chemistry: contact electrification and beyond
title: The hidden electric fields sitting at everyday interfaces
blurb: A review arguing that faint electric fields living at solid-liquid and other interfaces, set up by electron transfer and ion redistribution, can themselves drive chemical transformations. Reviews like this reframe more than they prove, but it is a useful lens if your reactions happen at a boundary.

original_title: From prediction to materials design: machine learning in electrocatalytic water splitting
title: Machine learning grows up a little for water-splitting catalysts
blurb: A review tracking machine learning in water-splitting electrocatalysis as it moves from static screening toward dynamic interfaces and interpretable models. The honest read is that interpretability and experimentally relevant are still aspirations more than deliverables here, but the direction is right.

original_title: Minimum effort, maximum effect: modulating twisted intramolecular charge transfer for ultralong room temperature phosphorescence
title: Wringing long room-temperature phosphorescence out of a single benzene
blurb: A twisted intramolecular charge-transfer strategy pushes ultralong room-temperature phosphorescence in minimalist single-benzene emitters, with roughly ten-fold gains in lifetime and efficiency. Impressive for such a stripped-down luminophore, though these systems tend to be fussy about matrix and moisture.

original_title: Role of the Fe-FeCl2 contact interface in promoting redox reversibility and electrochemical kinetics in Fe/FeCl2-graphite molten salt batteries
title: Why the Fe-FeCl2 contact makes a molten-salt battery behave
blurb: The Fe-FeCl2 interface gets the credit for the redox reversibility and low voltage hysteresis in these Fe/FeCl2-graphite molten-salt cells, by giving favorable Fe0 sites and better conductivity. A clean interfacial story is worth something, but molten-salt cells live or die on materials and temperature, and scale is another world.

original_title: Charged ligand-driven photoluminescence sensing enhancements in terbium-modified two-dimensional metal-organic frameworks
title: Charged ligands sharpening a terbium MOF into an ion sensor
blurb: Putting charged ligands on terbium-modified 2D zirconium MOFs boosts photoluminescent sensing of charged ions through plain electrostatics. A tidy design handle, and as ever with MOF sensors the questions are selectivity in a real matrix and whether it survives more than a few cycles.

original_title: Efficient targeting of human glial progenitor cells in vivo with engineered AAV vectors and glymphatic delivery
title: Engineered AAV that reaches glial cells through the brain's plumbing
blurb: AAV vectors delivered through the glymphatic system hit human glial progenitor cells broadly in vivo, which is a route around the usual problem of getting virus into the right brain cells. It is a vector-and-delivery result, so the therapeutic version still has to clear durability and safety, but the targeting itself is the news.

original_title: When Color Adds Nothing: A Causal Audit of Channel Triplication in Alzheimer's MRI Classification
title: Copying grayscale MRI into three color channels changes nothing
blurb: A preprint auditing the routine habit of triplicating a grayscale MRI into RGB before feeding a classifier, and finding it mostly does not move the predictions. The kind of unglamorous negative result that quietly saves people from cargo-culting a preprocessing step.

original_title: Hydrodynamic shear enables enrichment of functional tumor antigen-reactive T cells
title: Using shear force to fish out the tumor-reactive T cells
blurb: A microfluidic platform uses hydrodynamic shear to enrich the rare tumor-antigen-reactive T cells that adoptive therapies depend on and normally struggle to isolate. It is a preprint with a clever physical handle, and the real test is whether the cells it enriches actually perform, not just sort.

original_title: Are glucose monitors a good thing for 2-year-olds? Experts look at risks, benefits
title: An over-the-counter glucose monitor aimed at toddlers
blurb: The newly cleared over-the-counter CGM is being pitched at childhood obesity, and clinicians are pushing back on strapping a continuous sensor to a two-year-old. Worth reading for the risk-benefit argument, because more data and better outcomes are not the same thing in kids.

original_title: Behavioral and Functional Profiling of Acomys cahirinus Fibroblasts Reveals Enhanced Matrix Remodeling Capacity
title: What the spiny mouse's fibroblasts do differently when they heal
blurb: The African spiny mouse regenerates skin without scarring, and this preprint profiles its fibroblasts to find the enhanced matrix-remodeling behavior behind it. Mechanism-hunting in a niche model, and the leap from these cells remodel more to anything you could borrow for human healing is still large.

original_title: STAT+: Trump administration pursues more durable changes to science policy after setbacks in court
title: The administration goes after the rulebook under federal research funding
blurb: Reporting that the White House is trying to rewrite the base regulation behind federal contracts, which would let the executive branch direct research and terminate grants at will. Dry policy plumbing, but this is the layer that quietly decides what science gets funded.

original_title: Molecular Diradical Spin Qubits in a Crystalline Host as a Platform for Quantum Sensing
title: Molecular diradicals as spin qubits you can grow in a crystal
blurb: Diradical molecules hosted in a crystal act as spin qubits, pitched as a platform for quantum sensing. Molecular qubits are attractive because chemistry can tune them, and the perennial catch is coherence time surviving contact with a real, warm, noisy sample.

original_title: Sonification of Elephant Infrasound
title: Turning elephant infrasound into something you can hear
blurb: Elephants rumble below 20 Hz, under human hearing, and this preprint sonifies those calls so people can actually perceive them. More instrument than discovery, but a genuinely nice one for anyone who works with signals living outside the range we happen to sense.

original_title: Building Chemistry from “Lego” Blocks: Metal-Organic Frameworks after the 2025 Nobel Prize
title: Where metal-organic frameworks go now that they have a Nobel
blurb: A perspective taking stock of metal-organic frameworks after the 2025 chemistry Nobel put them in the spotlight. Reads as field-taking-stock rather than new results, useful if you want the honest version of what MOF chemistry has actually delivered instead of the press one.

original_title: Spectroscopic Evidence in Optical Absorption for the Formation and Transformation of Prenucleation Clusters of CdS Quantum Dots
title: Catching CdS quantum dots before they are quantum dots
blurb: Optical absorption used as evidence for prenucleation clusters forming and transforming on the way to CdS quantum dots, the stage nucleation theories usually argue about without watching. Indirect evidence by its nature, but pinning the early clusters spectroscopically is the useful part.

original_title: STAT+: Prime Medicine claims victory in dispute over gene-editing technology with Beam Therapeutics
title: Prime Medicine says it won its gene-editing fight with Beam
blurb: Prime Medicine claims an arbitration win over Beam Therapeutics in a dispute over gene-editing IP, two well-funded companies with overlapping technology. Arbitration outcomes are thin on public detail, so treat the victory framing as one side's until the terms actually surface.

original_title: Synthesis and in vivo evaluation of a bifunctional, glutamic acid derived chelator for the 203/212Pb theranostic pair
title: A lead chelator tuned by charge engineering
blurb: Glutamic acid-derived bifunctional chelator for Pb-203/212 theranostics, with charge optimization to improve pharmacokinetics. Charge engineering is a sensible design principle, though lead chelators are hardly in short supply.

original_title: Synthesis and evaluation of 4-(α-arylvinyl)pyridines as activated linker groups for application in antibody-drug conjugates.
title: Pyridine-based activated linkers for ADCs
blurb: 4-(α-Arylvinyl)pyridines evaluated as activated linker groups for antibody-drug conjugates. The scaffold is novel, but linker design is crowded enough that you'd need clear advantages to justify adoption.

original_title: Electroless semi-automatic synthesis of (225)Ac-labeled PSMA-derivatives for clinical application and evaluation of rechelation capability.
title: Automating Ac-225 PSMA synthesis without electrochemistry
blurb: Semi-automatic electroless radiosynthesis for Ac-225-PSMA compounds for clinical use. They're evaluating rechelation capability, which likely reflects concerns about chelate stability in vivo, though the actual results aren't in the title.

original_title: One-Pot Orthogonal Dual Functionalization of mi3 Self-Assembling Protein Nanoparticles via Sortase A and SpyCatcher/SpyTag Ligation
title: Simultaneous dual functionalization of mi3 nanoparticles
blurb: Sortase A and SpyCatcher/SpyTag enabling orthogonal modifications on mi3 in a single pot. Clean chemistry in principle, though whether mi3 actually gains traction as a platform is still an open bet.

original_title: Comparative evaluation of <sup>67</sup>Ga production via the <sup>67</sup>Zn and <sup>68</sup>Zn routes on proton cyclotrons: Yield, radionuclidic purity, and cooling-time behavior
title: Which cyclotron route makes cleaner Ga-67
blurb: Head-to-head comparison of Zn-67 and Zn-68 bombardment for Ga-67 on proton cyclotrons, tracking yield, purity, and cooling kinetics. Both routes are established, so this is optimization data rather than innovation, and recent enough (Oct 2026) that independent confirmation would help.

original_title: [ 68 Ga]Ga-DOTA-DP-UBI 29-41: A Novel 68 Ga-Labeled Ubiquicidin 29-41 Derivative Containing d -Proline for Bacterial Infection PET Imaging
title: A d-proline ubiquicidin variant for Ga-68 infection imaging
blurb: Ubiquicidin-29-41 modified with d-proline and labeled with Ga-68 for bacterial infection PET. Ubiquicidin is promising for infection imaging, but Ga-68's 68-minute half-life constrains the practical clinical window.

original_title: High-yield, standardised automated radiosynthesis process for routine clinical insulinoma PET with [⁶⁸Ga]Ga-NODAGA-exendin-4
title: Automated Ga-68 exendin synthesis for insulinoma PET
blurb: High-yield standardized automated procedure for Ga-68 NODAGA-exendin-4 in routine clinical use. Exendin-4 is already standard for insulinoma imaging, so the gain here is reproducibility and operational consistency.

original_title: Development and evaluation of improved in-house procedure for radiolabelling of theranostic agent [(177)Lu]Lu-PSMA-617.
title: Optimized Lu-177 PSMA-617 radiolabeling procedure
blurb: Refined in-house procedure for Lu-177 PSMA-617 labeling with evaluation of the improvements. PSMA-617 is standard-of-care, so this is incremental optimization where consistency in the clinic matters more than novelty.

original_title: Electrochemical Tryptophan-Selective Bioconjugation in Neutral Buffer via Cooperative N -Oxyl Radicals
title: Electrochemical tryptophan conjugation in neutral pH
blurb: N-Oxyl radicals generated electrochemically enabling tryptophan-selective bioconjugation under neutral conditions. Tryptophan reactivity is useful when tyrosines are off-limits, though selectivity in peptides with multiple Trp residues remains challenging.

original_title: Modular Assembly of Bioconjugates Enabled by a Pyridine-Based Chemoselective Sequential Conjugation Platform.
title: Sequential bioconjugation via pyridine chemistry
blurb: Pyridine-based platform for modular, chemoselective sequential bioconjugate assembly. Sequential conjugations are elegant in principle, but cumulative yields and reactivity between steps usually become the limiting factors.

original_title: Proximity-Driven Protein Ligation Beyond the Concentration Limit.
title: Ligating proteins without needing high local concentration
blurb: Proximity-driven protein ligation that works even when you dilute past the concentration floor where normal methods stall. Useful if you're working with scarce proteins or need to keep concentrations down, though how far below the usual limit you can actually go depends on the substrates you're using.

original_title: Novartis to pay $1.5B for Myricx Bio and its novel ADC payloads - BioWorld News
title: Novartis buys Myricx Bio's ADC payloads for $1.5B
blurb: Novartis acquiring Myricx Bio for 1.5 billion dollars to get their ADC payload technology. The bet is that novel payloads matter enough to justify the premium in a crowded conjugate market, though ADCs are already doing fine for Novartis.

original_title: Updated Recommendations for the Bioanalysis of Antibody-Drug Conjugates (ADC) from the ADC working group of the AAPS Bioanalytical Community.
title: Measuring ADCs: the AAPS bioanalysis guidance
blurb: Updated bioanalytical recommendations for antibody-drug conjugates from the AAPS working group, covering what you need to measure and how. Consensus work like this is usually solid, though it may lag what people are already doing in industry.

original_title: A Sweet and Stable Strike: Multivalent Glucosylated Gd-DOTBA Conjugate with Ultrahigh Relaxivity for Targeted MRI of Aggressive Cancers
title: A glucose-targeted gadolinium contrast agent
blurb: Multivalent glucosylated gadolinium conjugate designed for better MRI contrast in cancer imaging, using glucose uptake for targeting. High relaxivity in vitro, though whether improved contrast and selectivity hold in vivo is what actually matters.

original_title: Radiolabeling and physicochemical characterization of terbium-161 DOTATATE.
title: How to label and characterize terbium-161 DOTATATE
blurb: Methods for radiolabeling terbium-161 with DOTATATE and the physicochemical profile of the product. Tb-161 is chasing alpha therapy applications, but you need this basic characterization work before anything clinical can happen.

original_title: Fully GMP-complient radiosynthesis of [161Tb]Tb-PSMA-1 using the miniAiO synthesizer for clinical translation
title: Automated synthesis of clinical-grade terbium-161 PSMA-1
blurb: GMP-compliant radiosynthesis of Tb-161 PSMA-1 using the miniAiO automated platform, aimed at clinical translation. Automation and compliance in one move is the goal, though real-world throughput and yield are what clinical programs actually need.

original_title: Exclusively Chemoselective S‑Acylation for Peptide Radiolabeling Using [(18)F]Fluoronicotinic Acid 4‑Nitrophenyl Ester as a Prosthetic Compound.
title: Chemoselective fluorine-18 labeling for peptides
blurb: A fluoronicotinic prosthetic compound that labels peptides at cysteine with exclusive selectivity against nucleophiles like lysine. The clean selectivity is a practical win for radiosynthesis, though whether it saves time in practice depends on your peptide substrates.

original_title: Enzymatic Synthesis of Glyco-DNA Equipped with Oligosaccharides and Charged Monosaccharides
title: Enzymatic synthesis of glyco-DNA
blurb: Enzymatic approach to building DNA conjugates with oligosaccharides and charged monosaccharides attached. Enzymatic attachment offers cleaner control than chemical methods, though the practical payoff depends on whether the glycosylated DNA is stable and soluble enough to use.

original_title: Cycle-dependent variation of tumor absorbed dose rates in(177)Lu-DOTATATE therapies.
title: Tumor dose drifts cycle-to-cycle in Lu-177 DOTATATE therapy
blurb: Absorbed tumor dose from Lu-177 DOTATATE shows cycle-to-cycle variation in therapy, a pattern that affects how you predict outcome and tolerability. Understanding the variation matters for dose planning, though whether it's patient-specific noise or physiologic response is what actually drives clinical outcomes.

original_title: Metformin and renal dosimetry in patients undergoing [(177)Lu]Lu-PSMA therapy: reduced kidney absorbed dose without impact on tumor dosimetry.
title: Metformin cuts kidney dose in Lu-177 PSMA therapy
blurb: Patients on metformin during Lu-177 PSMA therapy showed reduced kidney absorbed dose without losing tumor uptake. Kidney toxicity limits PSMA therapy, so a straightforward intervention that spares the kidney is valuable, though replication in a prospective cohort would close the loop.
venue: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

original_title: Real-world evaluation of [(177)Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 RLT in post-taxane mCRPC: feasibility and safety of individualized treatment in a challenging cohort.
title: Lu-177 PSMA in heavily treated mCRPC
blurb: Real-world study of [(177)Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 in post-taxane castration-resistant prostate cancer with individualized dosing. It's feasible and safe in this population, though a small selected cohort is different from the broad run-of-the-mill case.
venue: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

original_title: Rapid assessment of target-binding fractions in molecular imaging agents using size-exclusion HPLC.
title: Quick check for binding efficiency in imaging agents
blurb: Size-exclusion HPLC as a rapid way to measure how much of your imaging agent actually binds the target. Simple enough for routine use, though it assumes the bound and unbound forms separate cleanly by size.

original_title: Affinity-Tuned Albumin Hitchhiking Extends the Bioorthogonal Capture Window in Pretargeting Radiotheranostics.
title: Albumin binding extends the capture window in pretargeted therapy
blurb: Tuning albumin affinity to stretch the timeframe for bioorthogonal capture in pretargeted radiotheranostics. The kinetics look better on paper, though the real question is whether the extra residence time actually improves tumor dosimetry or just adds background.

original_title: Development of [ 111 In]In-CHX-A″-DTPA-αCD68 for ImmunoSPECT to Image Murine Macrophages
title: A SPECT tracer for imaging macrophages
blurb: [(111)In]In-CHX-A″-DTPA-αCD68, an anti-CD68 tracer for macrophage imaging with SPECT. Preclinical so far, and cross-reactivity with other immune cells would be the first thing to nail down before scaling up.

original_title: Dose-Expansion Study of OBI-999, a Globo H-Targeting Antibody-Drug Conjugate, in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors.
title: Globo H-targeting ADC in advanced solid tumors
blurb: Dose-expansion study of OBI-999, an antibody-drug conjugate targeting Globo H, in advanced solid tumors. Phase 1b data only, so it's too early to know if this one actually lands, but Globo H is a reasonable target if they can hit it cleanly.

original_title: Technetium-99m Radiolabeled Carboplatin Polymeric Nanoparticles for Imaging and Treatment of Epithelial Ovarian Cancer.
title: Carboplatin nanoparticles labeled for imaging and therapy
blurb: Tc-99m labeling of carboplatin-loaded polymeric nanoparticles for simultaneous imaging and treatment in ovarian cancer. The theranostic angle is appealing, though nanoparticle pharmacokinetics are notoriously messy and you need to see actual accumulation before the imaging part matters.

original_title: Novel antibody-drug conjugate eliminates residual cancer cells in majority of patients with B-cell ALL - Medical Xpress
title: ADC clears residual disease in most B-cell ALL patients
blurb: An antibody-drug conjugate eliminated residual cancer cells in the majority of B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia patients in early trials. Company data for now, so real confirmation will come from independent replication, but the approach of targeting minimal residual disease is sound if the numbers hold.

original_title: Topological data analysis for antibody-drug conjugate payload discovery: a computational framework for mechanistic classification and target validation.
title: Topological data analysis for ADC payload discovery
blurb: A computational framework using topological data analysis for ADC payload classification and target validation. Methods paper, so the question is whether the approach actually speeds up discovery or just reorganizes what you already knew.

original_title: Limitations of Mono-Exponential Individual Fitting as a Reference Model for Single-Time-Point Dosimetry in [(177)Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 Therapy.
title: Single-time-point dosimetry in Lu-177 PSMA has hidden assumptions
blurb: Mono-exponential kinetic fitting doesn't hold up as a reference model for single-time-point dosimetry in [(177)Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 therapy. The clearance is more complex than the model assumes, so you're probably wrong if you're predicting dose from one snapshot and a simplified curve.

original_title: Enhanced pre-therapy dosimetry for SSTR PET: A head-to-head preclinical assessment of [(89)Zr]Zr-DFO-TOC and [(89)Zr]Zr-DFO-TATE.
title: Comparing Zr-89 tracers for somatostatin receptor dosimetry
blurb: Head-to-head preclinical comparison of [(89)Zr]Zr-DFO-TOC and [(89)Zr]Zr-DFO-TATE for pre-therapy PET dosimetry in somatostatin receptor imaging. Preclinical data only, and the choice between them might come down to practical factors like shelf-life and production yield rather than imaging performance alone.

original_title: Novartis scales ADC portfolio with $1.5bn Myricx acquisition - BioXconomy
title: Novartis spends $1.5bn on Myricx to expand ADCs
blurb: An acquisition to add to the ADC portfolio, priced at $1.5bn. Steep for pure portfolio work, so there's presumably something strategic in Myricx's development pipeline.

original_title: De Novo Lipid Switch Design for Reactive Oxygen Species-Triggered Liposomal Release
title: Lipid switches that open when cells generate ROS
blurb: De novo lipid design to trigger liposomal release when ROS appears. Reasonable as a tumor-targeting strategy, though ROS-activated triggers have seen plenty of research with minimal clinical adoption so far.

original_title: Development of a Freeze-Dried Bevacizumab Kit for (99m)Tc Radiolabeling and Preclinical Evaluation In Vitro and in Normal Mice.
title: A freeze-dried kit for (99m)Tc-labeled bevacizumab
blurb: A freeze-dried formulation to make (99m)Tc radiolabeling of bevacizumab easier. Preclinical work so far, so the step to humans is still ahead.

original_title: Development and evaluation of improved in-house procedure for radiolabelling of theranostic agent [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617
title: An improved protocol for [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 synthesis
blurb: Automated synthesis of [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 with improved procedures. Incremental gains, but those add up in translational radiopharma.

original_title: Fully GMP-complient radiosynthesis of [(161)Tb]Tb-PSMA-1 using the miniAiO synthesizer for clinical translation.
title: GMP-grade (161)Tb-PSMA synthesis on a benchtop platform
blurb: Clinical-grade synthesis of [(161)Tb]Tb-PSMA-1 automated on the miniAiO. Terbium-161 is less established than lutetium-177 clinically, so the adoption curve remains uncertain.

original_title: Aggregation Issues in Radioactive Copper-Labeled Antibodies.
title: Copper-labeled antibodies have an aggregation problem
blurb: Radioactive copper labels on antibodies tend to aggregate. A real hurdle if copper is going to be a viable option for antibody imaging.

original_title: Preclinical evaluation of a fluorine-18-labeled indocyanine green analog for PET imaging of hepatic function
title: An (18)F-labeled indocyanine green for liver PET
blurb: Indocyanine green with a (18)F label for PET imaging of liver function instead of optical detection. Preclinical, and whether the liver selectivity of the original dye carries over to the radiolabeled version remains to be shown.

original_title: Altruist Biologics' Hangzhou Antibody-Drug Conjugate Facility Receives Provincial Regulatory Approval for Segmented Production - PR Newswire
title: Altruist's Hangzhou ADC facility clears regulatory approval
blurb: Provincial regulatory approval in China for segmented ADC production. A foothold in Chinese manufacturing, though segmented production suggests they're not yet running the full synthesis pipeline independently.

original_title: Convo-1α Produces Durable Responses in Patients Previously Treated With PSMA Radioligand Therapy - HMP Global Learning Network
title: Convo-1α shows activity in PSMA-pretreated patients
blurb: Early data on Convo-1α in patients who've exhausted PSMA radioligand therapy. Press release so far, so the durable response claims need independent verification.

original_title: World's First Bispecific Antibody-Drug Conjugate (ADC) Approved, but Commercial Viability Remains to Be Tested - 富途牛牛
title: First bispecific ADC approved, market proof is another thing
blurb: A bispecific antibody conjugate clears approval as a technical milestone. Bispecific engineering adds cost and complexity, so the clinical edge over conventional ADCs will need to be real enough to drive adoption.
